method: EXPLORATORY threshold sweep: run a simple rolling z-score over 200 synthetic windows with known ground truth (50% drift injected at 3.5-8 sigma magnitude) to sanity-check threshold behaviour. Actual z-score computation, not simulated confidence. This is NOT the production detector.
circular_reasoning_fix: Previous version used random uniform confidence (self-grading). Now: generate baseline data -> inject real drift -> run rolling z-score -> count TP/FP/TN/FN.
production_note: This sweep runs on CLEAN synthetic data, so even a 3.0 threshold shows ~0 FP here. The production engine does NOT use a plain rolling z-score at 3.0 sigma: it uses an EWMA control chart (alpha=0.3) whose operating point K=4.0 was selected on the REAL holdout per docs/05_adrs.md ADR-006. On the real holdout K=3.0 gives FP 17.5% (fails the <=12% gate) while K=4.0 gives FP 7.1%. Treat the sweep below as a directional sanity-check, not the production threshold.
brier_interpretation: Brier score < 0.25 = good calibration. Lower = better.
